not so much as i conceive in regard of growth or declination , as in regard of progression , make the infancy thereof from Adam to Noah , the childhood from Noah to Abraham , the youth from Abraham to David , the man estate from David to Christ , the old age from Christ to the end of it . and as the duration in all the other age of man be certain , but the last of old age uncertain : so be it in the world . and as Chrysostome well note , we call not the end of the year the last hour , or day or week thereof , but the last month or quarter : so we call this last age of the world the end thereof . but how long this age shall last , it be still doubtful , it be one of those secret which the almighty have lock up in the cabinet of his own counsel , a secret which be neither possible neither profitable for we to know , as be not by God reveal unto we in his word , much less than in the book of nature .

Mim . to the reader . as contemplation in general , be a pure act of the soul ; so contemplation of divine thing , be most proper unto it . this be the heavenly manna , whereon it feed ; hereby it grow , hereby it be loosen from the knot of flesh and blood ; hereby it mount above the narrow confine and limit , above the sordide thing of this world , above either the threat or promise thereof ; and both return to the original from whence it come , and appli it self to the end whither with swift celerity it pace . and this be a invincible argument of the divine nature of the soul , if it take delight in matter divine ; if it be busy therein , not as in other man affair , but as in the own . assure , in nothing else man be so much man , as in contemplation of heavenly thing . oh how contemptible a thing be man , if he advance not above humane cogitation ? but conceive here with ( gentle reader ) that contemplation be but a fruitless action of the soul , unless it be thereby inflame to love . contemplation be a work of the understanding , and have no perfection in it self ▪ but be a way and mean to some degree of perfection , by elevate the will to a divine love and union with God . the understanding give not food to the soul , but prepare it only for the taste of the will . good be the proper object of the will . and therefore when the will find a infinite depth of goodness in God , it shall|be exceed icy , if it burn not like a phoenix , in the fire of divine love , which by contemplation be therein kindle . wherefore , if thou will contemplate sweet and profitable , erect the force of thy will , to love that which thou do understand : for otherwise , thou shall be but a cold and curious speculator ; thou shall never arrive at the true end of contemplation . for the end of contemplate be not in speculation of the understanding , but in a ardent love of the will . now because of all divine thing none be more noble than the work of our redemption ; none wherein God so much manifest his goodness , and consequent , none so fit to inflame our love : i have address my sabbath exercise this present year , to this prayer of Christ upon the cross for his enemy . hope notwithstanding , that the residue of the penitential psalm , not yet comprise in my David tear , shall hereafter be in the like manner prosecute ; either by my self ( if life continue ) or by some other more happy and industrious hand . assure , man be too mortal , either to handle or to know thing immortal : as we know but in part , so we can express but a part of what we know ; and must have many partner in our labour therein . a man shall so endeavour to live , as he may do good to all : but if that can be , yet to many : and if not that , yet to some : and if not that , at least to himself . my desire hereby be to do good to all ; even to he , who prefrict malice have much wrong both i and himself ; in case he have either conscience or shame to be wound by a last memory of his dishonest deal . but albeit , i esteem both these to be almost impossible alike ; yet i make no doubt , but hereby i shall do good to many : and the rather , because many have acknowledge to i great good , which they have receive by my former write in this kind . but as in the same meadow , the ox seek grass , the hound a hare , and the stork a lizard : so i expect that in this work some will hunt after conceit , some after cavil ; but many will find good pasture for their soul . of these i expect no other recompense , but their prayer for i ; of which i acknowledge i stand much in need . hom . ii nonlatinalphabet nonlatinalphabet tam gravis ille mihi nigri quam limb inaditis ; his body much enfeeble with fast and watch all the night before , and with the buffet and scourge which he endure : be much exhaust thereby , both of spirit and blood : here the Jew first unburden he of his cross , prepare the hole for fasten he thereto ; bring the hammer and the nail before his face ; his enemy cluster the while about he , and not spare to vex he with many opprobrious and blasphemous speech . then they hasty pull off his apparel ; his strip which cleave fast to his body , by reason of the congeal blood that issue from he when he be scourge ; tear open his wound , and rend away some part of his skin and flesh . so he stand naked and bloody a while ; and than they rude throw he upon the altar of the cross lie upon the ground . his nail . and apply the backside of one of his hand to a hole which they have prepare in the cross , Psal. 71. they smite the rock and river gush forth : they nail his hand to the cross , and blood stream abundant from the wound .

Lenoricensis , who style himself a servant of God , and a apostle of Jesus Christ , in his epistle and burdigal . write thus : you heretofore honour the priest , which deceive you with their sacrifice , which they offer to dumb and deaf image , that neither can help you , nor themselves : but now much more you ought to honour the priest of almighty God , who minister life unto you in the cup , and live bread . by this argument of marshal , the romish priest , that give the people but a half communion , shall lose half of the honour due unto God priest , if not the whole . for thus out of marshal premise i conclude . those , and none but those priest be to be honour and reverence , who administer life to the people in the cup : the romish priest administer not life to the people in the cup : therefore they be not to be reverence , or honour . anno 92. third , Clemens nonlatinalphabet . in his second book of constitution , 57 chapter thus enjoin : after the offer of the sacrifice , let every order a part receive the body of our lord , and his precious blood . anno 100 four , Ignatius , the scholar of saint John the Euangelist , bishop of Antioch and Martyr , in his epistle to the philadelphians , enforce a argument to unity from the communion : i exhort you to embrace one faith , one manner of preach , and use of the sacrament of the lord supper : for the flesh of our lord Jesus be one , and his blood one that be shed for we , there be one bread also break for all , nonlatinalphabet . and one cup distribute unto all . Bellarmine his first answer . Bellarmine be put to a miserable plunge in his answer to this allegation . first , he say , in the latin bell . the sacr . eucharist . lib . 4. cap . 26. in latinis codicibus non habetur ; vnus calix omnibus distributus ; sed vnus calix totius Ecclesiae . nec multum fidendum graecis codicibus . copy the word of Ignatius be not as we cite they ; there be one cup distribute unto all : but there be one cup of the whole church : and though the greek copy read as we do , yet he say , that much credit be not to be give to they . the refutation . against this answer i reply : first , that if we may not trust the greek edition of Ignatius , much less may we trust the latin translation ; especial since of late they be come into huckster hand . to appeal from a translation to the original be usual : but to appeal from the original to a translation be a thing unheard of . this be to make the brook or stream to be pure than the fountain or spring . the poet teach Bellarmine another lesson : dulciùs ex ipso fonte bibuntur aquae . Ignatius , as it be well know , write in greek , and therefore unless Bellarmine can prove , that other greek copy agree with his latin translation , and not with we , he speak nothing to the purpose : for a translation be of no credit further , than it agree with the and howsoever Bellarmine may produce some latin copy , that translate the word of Ignatius , as Bellarmine set they down , vnus Calix totius Ecclesiae ; yet Uitlemeus , and diverse other latin copy follow the original verbatim , render they thus , vnus calix omnibus distributus , that be , one cup distribute unto all . original . second , even Bellarmine corrupt translation make against the church of Rome , and prove , that the practice in Ignatius his time be for the whole church to communicate in both kind : for why else call he it , calicem totius Ecclesiae , the cup of the whole church ? Ignatius there speak not of the possession , but of the use of the cup : and if the priest only have drink of it , he will have call it the priest cup : but in term it the cup of the whole church , he plain signify , that the whole church use it in the celebration of the lord supper . Bellarmine say , that the force of Ignatius his argument consist in the unity of the cup , and not in the universality of they that drink , for he exhort there to unity . the refutation . first , Ignatius exhort there all to unity , because all eat of one bread , and drink of one cup . his argument therefore stand both in the universality of they that drink , and the unity of the cup : and it may be thus reduce into form . all that eat of one bread , and drink of one holy cup , in remembrance of one body offer , and one blood shed for all , aught to embrace unity : but all you of the church of Philadelphia ( people as well as priest ) eat of one bread , and drink of one holy cup , in memory of one body offer , and one blood of Christ shed for you all : therefore all you of the church of Philadelphia ought to embrace unity , and godly love . if the pinch , or strain of the argument be in unity only , it will not hold ; for if some only drink of this cup , and not other , this shall rather make more for a division , then for unity ; it be the communion of more in one , that Ignatius lay for the ground of his argument , enforce unity . second , howsoever the argument stand , it make no great matter ; sith we insist not so much upon the argument it self , as upon that his express affirmation ; that one cup in his time be give unto all . this assertion alone sufficient prove the practice of the church in his time . Bellarmine say , that nothing can be enforce from these word of Ignatius , but that it be the use in that time , when there be but few Christians to give the cup unto all : but this be a example , it be no precept : so the cardinal . the refutation . first , it be not true , which he here affirm ; that there be but few Christians in Ignatius his time : for all history of those time , and the epistle of Ignatius testify the contrary , and in this very church of Philadelphia , the holy ghost testify , Apoc. 3. 8. that there be many Christians . behold , i have set before thou a open door , and no man shall shut it , etc second , though the primitive church be not of that large extent , as the church in succeed age : yet the authority of the church in that age , in which the apostle live , and their immediate successor , be far great , than in any late age . third , in this last answer the cardinal yield we the cause ; for we cite these word of Ignatius only to prove the practice of the primitive church , and thus much Bellarmine confess , whereupon i add ; that this confess practice of the primitive church be ground on our lord precept : drink you all of this ; for the church so near Christ can be suppose to have swerve any way from his institution , by add any thing unto it , or take away from it : certain , Ignatius , and the church , wherein he bear sway , observe the order and practice of saint John his master ; and if saint John administer the cup in all church to the people , so do the rest of the apostle ; for they vary not from Christ , or among themselves in celebrate the lord supper . and what the apostle do joint , no Christian doubt , but they do by the direction of the holy ghost , accord to our lord will and commandment . and thus we see this example amount to a precept , and the practice in Ignatius his time , aught to be a precedent for all future time . sect . ii testimony of the practice of the christian church in the second age . from 100 to 200. anno Dom. 150.
